perturbation.original_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to some, but not all, of the securities of the above referenced issuer. Inclusion of this Agreement as an Exhibit to such filing is optional.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by first stating that the agreement is NOT in accordance with Rule 13d-1(k), then stating that joint filing is only for some of the securities, directly contradicting the original intent to cover ALL securities, and finally, that it's optional to include the agreement as an exhibit. This contradicts the later statement that the agreement is to be included as an exhibit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text states the agreement is in accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) and covers ALL securities, requiring the agreement to be included as an exhibit. The changed text states that the agreement is NOT in accordance with Rule 13d-1(k), covers only SOME securities, and inclusion as an exhibit is optional. This directly contradicts the original intent to cover ALL securities and the mandatory inclusion of the agreement, creating an inconsistency regarding which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in a single original. Digital copies are permitted for convenience, but only the originally signed document shall be considered legally binding.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement may only be executed in a single original, contradicting the original text stating it can be executed in any number of counterparts, each being deemed an original. This introduces uncertainty regarding which version is legally binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement may be executed in a single original, directly contradicting the contradicted text (which is also the original text) that states the agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each deemed an original. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the validity of multiple signed copies, which aligns with the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement as of July 6, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned may execute this Agreement; the effective date will be determined individually by each signatory.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by replacing 'hereby execute' with 'may execute', implying that signing is not mandatory and 'as of July 6, 2020' with 'the effective date will be determined individually by each signatory', creating ambiguity about when the agreement actually becomes effective and binding, which contradict the fact that should be on July 6, 2020.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement as of July 6, 2020.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the effective date of the agreement, contradicting the original, which specifies a fixed date of July 6, 2020. By allowing each signatory to determine the effective date individually, the changed text directly clashes with the contradicted text, leading to uncertainty and a potential conflict in enforcement. The original text requires a single, unified effective date, while the changed text permits multiple effective dates, which creates an inconsistency as per the definition provided. The agreement can't simultaneously be effective for all parties on July 6, 2020, and on different dates as determined by each signatory.
